cause: Infestation with Otodectes cynotis mites that live in the ear canal and feed on skin debris.
warning: Highly contagious between cats and other pets. Untreated infestations can lead to secondary ear infections.

medicines.name: Selamectin
medicines.note: Common veterinary treatment for ear mites.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: Supports skin health and recovery from irritation.

supplements.name: Vitamin E
supplements.benefit: Supports skin repair and immune function.

safety_warnings: Treat all pets in the household if advised by a veterinarian.
Do not insert cotton swabs deep into the ear canal.

cause: Infestation by fleas acquired from other animals or the environment.
warning: Severe infestations may cause anemia in kittens.
safety_warnings: Never use dog flea products containing permethrin on cats.
Treat the environment as well as the cat.

cause: Dermatophyte fungal infection, commonly Microsporum canis.
warning: Highly contagious to humans and other animals.
safety_warnings: Wear gloves when handling infected animals.
Disinfect bedding and grooming equipment.

cause: Follicular plugging and bacterial overgrowth, often affecting the chin.
warning: Severe cases can become infected and painful.
safety_warnings: Switch plastic bowls to stainless steel or ceramic.

cause: Commonly caused by feline herpesvirus or calicivirus infection.
warning: Cats that stop eating can deteriorate quickly.
safety_warnings: Seek veterinary care if breathing difficulty develops.

cause: Inflammation and narrowing of the lower airways.
warning: Open-mouth breathing is an emergency.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: May support reduction of inflammatory responses.

safety_warnings: Avoid cigarette smoke and strong fragrances.

cause: Accumulation of swallowed hair in the gastrointestinal tract.
warning: Repeated vomiting without hairballs may indicate another disease.

supplements.name: Hairball Control Fiber Supplement
supplements.benefit: Supports movement of ingested hair through the gut.

safety_warnings: Frequent grooming helps reduce hairballs.

cause: Inflammation, crystals, stones, stress-related cystitis, or urethral obstruction.
warning: Male cats unable to pass urine require immediate emergency treatment.
safety_warnings: Complete urinary blockage is life-threatening.

cause: Infection, irritation, allergy, or viral disease.
warning: Cloudy eye or severe pain requires urgent veterinary attention.
safety_warnings: Do not use human eye medications without veterinary advice.

cause: Bacterial infection following a bite or puncture wound.
warning: Abscesses often rupture and require veterinary treatment.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress
topical_treatments.use: May help drainage before veterinary treatment.

supplements.name: Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Supports gut health during antibiotic use.

safety_warnings: Do not squeeze or lance abscesses at home.

cause: Inflammation of the gums caused by plaque, tartar, bacteria, or underlying viral disease.
warning: Cats often hide dental pain. Reduced eating may be the first sign.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: May support gum health and reduce inflammation.

safety_warnings: Professional dental cleaning may be required.

cause: Progressive bacterial infection affecting gums, ligaments, and supporting tooth structures.
warning: Untreated disease can result in tooth loss and systemic complications.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: May support gum health and reduce inflammation.

safety_warnings: Routine dental examinations are recommended.

cause: Severe immune-mediated inflammation of oral tissues.
warning: Can be extremely painful and significantly affect eating.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: May help support management of chronic inflammation.

safety_warnings: Requires veterinary diagnosis and management.

cause: Progressive destruction of tooth structure, common in adult cats.
warning: One of the most painful feline dental diseases.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: May support oral comfort and reduce inflammation.

safety_warnings: Often requires dental extraction.

cause: Insufficient insulin activity leading to elevated blood glucose.
warning: Untreated diabetes can lead to diabetic ketoacidosis.

supplements.name: High-Protein Veterinary Diet
supplements.benefit: Helps maintain lean muscle mass and glucose control.

safety_warnings: Insulin should only be used under veterinary supervision.

cause: Excessive production of thyroid hormone, usually due to benign thyroid enlargement.
warning: Common in senior cats.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: Supports overall health in senior cats.

safety_warnings: Regular blood monitoring is required.

cause: Progressive loss of kidney function, commonly seen in older cats.
warning: Early diagnosis can significantly improve quality of life.
safety_warnings: Prescription kidney diets are commonly recommended.

cause: Various disorders affecting liver function including infection, inflammation, toxins, and hepatic lipidosis.
warning: Jaundice is a medical emergency.
safety_warnings: Yellow discoloration requires urgent veterinary evaluation.

cause: Dehydration, low fiber intake, pain, or underlying disease.
warning: Prolonged constipation can progress to megacolon.
safety_warnings: Seek veterinary care if no stool is passed for several days.

cause: Dietary changes, infection, parasites, stress, or gastrointestinal disease.
warning: Kittens can become dehydrated rapidly.
safety_warnings: Blood in stool requires veterinary attention.

cause: Inflammation of the pancreas. Can be associated with obesity, diabetes, inflammatory bowel disease, infections, or occur without a clear cause.
warning: Can become life-threatening if severe dehydration or systemic inflammation develops.

supplements.name: Probiotic
supplements.benefit: May support oral and immune health.

safety_warnings: Do not attempt home treatment for severe pancreatitis.

cause: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines caused by infection, dietary indiscretion, parasites, toxins, or food intolerance.
warning: Kittens may become dehydrated rapidly.
safety_warnings: Persistent vomiting requires veterinary attention.

cause: Immune reaction to dietary proteins or ingredients.
warning: Diagnosis requires a veterinary elimination diet trial.
safety_warnings: Food allergy diagnosis requires strict dietary control.

cause: Allergic reaction to environmental allergens such as pollen, dust mites, or mold.
warning: Secondary skin infections are common.
safety_warnings: Persistent itching should be evaluated by a veterinarian.

cause: Bacterial infection of the skin, often secondary to allergies, parasites, or wounds.
warning: Underlying causes should be identified to prevent recurrence.
safety_warnings: Do not stop antibiotics early.

cause: Infection with intestinal roundworms.
warning: Can be transmitted to humans.
safety_warnings: Practice good hygiene when handling feces.

cause: Parasitic infection of the small intestine.
warning: Severe infections may cause anemia.

supplements.name: Iron Support Supplement
supplements.benefit: May support recovery from veterinarian-confirmed anemia.

safety_warnings: Heavy infestations require veterinary treatment.

cause: Intestinal parasite commonly transmitted through fleas.
warning: Flea control is essential to prevent reinfection.
safety_warnings: Treat flea infestations simultaneously.

cause: Mosquito-transmitted parasitic infection affecting the heart and lungs.
warning: Even a small number of worms can cause severe disease in cats.
safety_warnings: Preventive medication is preferred over treatment.

cause: Infestation with Notoedres cati mites.
warning: Highly contagious to other cats.
safety_warnings: Treat all exposed cats if advised by a veterinarian.

cause: Retroviral infection primarily transmitted through deep bite wounds from infected cats.
warning: FIV-positive cats can live for many years with proper care and monitoring.
safety_warnings: Requires lifelong veterinary monitoring.

cause: Contagious retroviral infection affecting immune system and bone marrow.
warning: FeLV increases risk of cancer and severe infections.
safety_warnings: Vaccination is available for at-risk cats.

cause: Mutation of feline coronavirus leading to a severe systemic disease.
warning: Early diagnosis significantly improves treatment outcomes.
safety_warnings: Immediate veterinary evaluation recommended.

cause: Common viral infection causing respiratory and eye disease.
warning: Stress may trigger recurrence.
safety_warnings: Maintain hydration and food intake.

cause: Highly contagious viral respiratory disease.
warning: Some strains can cause severe systemic illness.
safety_warnings: Vaccination helps reduce disease severity.

cause: Damage to the corneal surface due to trauma, infection, or chronic irritation.
warning: Untreated ulcers may lead to blindness.
safety_warnings: Eye emergencies require prompt treatment.

cause: Inflammation inside the eye often associated with infection, trauma, cancer, or immune disease.
warning: Can result in blindness if untreated.
safety_warnings: Immediate ophthalmic examination recommended.

cause: Increased pressure within the eye causing optic nerve damage.
warning: Permanent blindness can occur rapidly.
safety_warnings: Emergency veterinary attention required.

cause: Bacterial infection affecting the urinary tract.
warning: Symptoms may resemble FLUTD.

supplements.name: Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Supports gut health during antibiotic therapy.

safety_warnings: Urine culture may be required.

cause: Mineral crystal formation within the urinary tract.
warning: May cause urinary obstruction, especially in male cats.

supplements.name: Prescription Urinary Diet
supplements.benefit: May help manage certain stone types.

safety_warnings: Complete urinary blockage is a life-threatening emergency.

cause: Can result from epilepsy, toxins, head trauma, liver disease, infections, metabolic disorders, or brain tumors.
warning: A seizure lasting longer than 5 minutes is a life-threatening emergency.
safety_warnings: Do not place hands inside the cat's mouth during a seizure.
Emergency veterinary care required for prolonged seizures.

cause: Poorly understood neurological and behavioral condition affecting skin sensitivity.
warning: May be confused with seizures or skin disease.
safety_warnings: Veterinary evaluation is recommended to rule out other diseases.

cause: Degenerative joint disease commonly affecting older cats.
warning: Cats often hide pain, making diagnosis difficult.
safety_warnings: Never give human pain medications to cats.

cause: Exposure to excessive environmental heat leading to dangerous body temperature elevation.
warning: Heatstroke can rapidly become fatal.
safety_warnings: Seek emergency veterinary care immediately.

cause: Exposure to toxic substances including medications, chemicals, toxic plants, or household products.
warning: Many toxins are fatal if treatment is delayed.
safety_warnings: Never induce vomiting unless instructed by a veterinarian.

cause: Physical injury resulting from falls, vehicle accidents, animal attacks, or other trauma.
warning: Internal injuries may not be immediately visible.
safety_warnings: Urgent veterinary examination recommended.

cause: Injection of venom from a venomous snake bite.
warning: Rapid treatment significantly improves survival.
safety_warnings: Emergency veterinary care required immediately.

cause: Environmental changes, conflict with other animals, lack of enrichment, or stressful events.
warning: Chronic stress may contribute to FLUTD and behavioral problems.
safety_warnings: Behavioral changes should be medically evaluated.

cause: Excess calorie intake combined with inadequate physical activity.
warning: Obesity increases risk of diabetes, arthritis, and urinary disease.
safety_warnings: Weight loss should occur gradually under veterinary guidance.

cause: No apparent disease detected.
warning: This profile should only be selected when no disease indicators are present.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
supplements.benefit: Supports skin, coat, and cardiovascular health.

safety_warnings: Regular veterinary wellness examinations are still recommended.
